title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: More than 60 international tall ships and military vessels sailed into Boston Harbor for the six-day Sail Boston 250 event.
The USS Constitution led the Parade of Sail with vessels from over 20 countries arriving from New York.
Thousands of visitors attended to celebrate Boston's maritime history, with ships docked at various city waterfronts.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner from UNC as the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, a 21-year-old Janesville native, batted .356 with six homers and 50 RBI in 68 games at UNC.
Defensively, Schaffner committed only three errors in 2026, leading to a .987 fielding percentage at shortstop.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Grand Parade of Sail 2026, led by the USS Constitution, showcases historic tall ships in Boston.
East Boston native Carol Zitano reflects on the cultural significance and personal memories of Tall Ships.
Sail Boston event highlights smaller replica vessels like the Bluenose Two and Schooner Adventure.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was named the 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor first-half performance.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts, allowing 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings for the Red Sox.
Despite his struggles as a starter, Bello excelled in relief with a 0.71 ERA and a 0.91 WHIP.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox face decision whether to buy or sell as MLB trade deadline approaches.
Boston's recent 7-game win streak puts them within two games of a wildcard spot.
Past indecision at trade deadlines has led to underwhelming results for the team.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira's concert at TD Garden marked her return to music after a seven-year hiatus with a two-hour performance.
The show featured hits like 'Hips Don't Lie' and 'Whenever, Wherever,' combining nostalgia with new work from her latest album.
Shakira's performance included elaborate stage setups and costumes, emphasizing themes of resilience and feminine strength.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Massachusetts awarded $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 new homes in six Gateway Cities.
Governments aim to convert historic buildings in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
Tax credits support the state's effort to address housing shortages and revitalize downtown areas.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested by a SWAT team in Albany, NY, for alleged armed bank robberies.
Sawyer is accused of robbing banks in Nashua, NH on June 12, and Boonton, NJ on June 27.
The arrest was part of the FBI's Operation Summer Heat 2.0 targeting violent crime, involving multi-state agencies.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead along Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, after being missing since July 4.
Grondin's body and his motorcycle were discovered by a resident who had been searching for him on Thursday night.
Preliminary investigation reveals Grondin crashed after crossing lanes and striking a post; no other vehicles involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
The Vermont-born singer, Noah Kahan, headlined four sold-out shows at Fenway Park.
Kahan is recognized for his music and advocacy for mental health awareness in New England.
